nodes.label: AI as literature discovery tool
nodes.grounding_span: I can spend hours combing through research trying to find what I'm looking for, or I can ask a model that will give me almost instant answers

nodes.label: AI as systematic troubleshooter
nodes.label_negative: Sole reliance on own or coworker knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: if a test is going wrong and I just want to talk through different troubleshooting stuff that my coworkers also don't have answers for- I'll use ChatGPT there as well

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I put in my rough draft and had the model give me suggestions for improvements

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: a lot of times that it wont cite a paper correctly or will be wrong on things

nodes.label: AI as tool for critical thinking about AI
nodes.grounding_span: it's really necessary for the user to have some common sense and logical thinking and not just blindly trust a model

nodes.label: AI for automated data analysis from raw fluorescence data
nodes.grounding_span: If we could get a model to be able to just receive the raw data and immediately analyze it into a graph

nodes.label: Institutional permission for AI use
nodes.label_negative: Unrestricted third-party access to unpublished data
nodes.grounding_span: a contract or some other understanding with the universities would need to be necessary before they'd allow raw unpublished scientific data to be handed over to a third party

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: maybe those ideas will be something that I absolutely should have thought of but just couldn't find in my brain

nodes.label: Scientific communication clarity
nodes.grounding_span: see where things were the least understandable

nodes.label: Data sovereignty and institutional trust
nodes.grounding_span: our research doesn't even belong to us, it's always the property of our university

nodes.label: Negative stance toward AI for literature search
nodes.grounding_span: It made it a lot easier to find where the gaps were in research rather than doing all that searching manually

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for debugging
nodes.grounding_span: I'll use ChatGPT there as well

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as editing aid
nodes.grounding_span: It's helpful for editing and revising

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as a tool
nodes.grounding_span: Sure, there's a lot of times that it wont cite a paper correctly or will be wrong on things, but it's a great starting point

nodes.label: Concern about privacy with AI
nodes.grounding_span: If we could get a model (that's allowed by the university to handle our data) to be able to just receive the raw data and immediately analyze it

nodes.label: Concern about institutional data privacy
nodes.grounding_span: the privacy constraint would be super necessary to think about as a company

nodes.label: Pragmatic tool selection based on effectiveness
Domain expertise as primary filter
nodes.grounding_span: it's really necessary for the user to have some common sense and logical thinking and not just blindly trust a model
